questions.question: The main aim of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) is:

propositions.A: To create new Language Learning Models
propositions.C: To create machines that are as smart as humans
propositions.D: To improve the capabilities of digital computers

questions.justification: The text states AGI's 'ultimate ambition... is to produce a machine whose overall intellectual ability is indistinguishable from that of a human being's' — exactly what C says.

questions.question: In paragraph three, the writer implies that:

propositions.B: A bunch of jobs will disappear because of ChatGPT
propositions.C: Computers will forcibly replace human beings
propositions.D: Experts are obliged to use ChatGPT

questions.justification: Paragraph three explains ChatGPT 'disturbed academics, journalists and others' because its writing is indistinguishable from human writing — a challenge to writing-related occupations, not a claim that jobs will literally vanish (B) or that machines will replace humans (C).

questions.question: Which of the following ideas is not developed in the text:
questions.justification: The text defines AI (paragraph 1), discusses drawbacks (deepfakes, hallucinations) and ethical/socioeconomic risks — but never discusses the speed of AI's decision-making.

questions.question: Which statement about AI is INCORRECT:

propositions.A: It's impossible to detect a fake replica generated by AI
propositions.B: AI will definitely replace man and his creativity
propositions.C: AI robots never give incorrect answers

questions.justification: The text describes an algorithm that DOES detect fake replicas (contradicting A), never claims AI will 'definitely' replace human creativity (contradicting B), and explicitly discusses hallucinations, i.e. AI giving inaccurate answers (contradicting C). All three statements are incorrect.

questions.question: AI has all these abilities except:

propositions.B: Performing complex tasks such as medical diagnosis
propositions.C: Showing more efficiency than humans in domains like speeding and data analysis
propositions.D: Understanding languages and complex situations, showing empathy and coming up with new ideas

questions.justification: The text is explicit that language models 'do not actually understand language as humans do' — genuine understanding and empathy are exactly what the text says AI lacks.

questions.question: According to the text, what is the hardest problem of AI?

propositions.A: It doesn't elaborate pertinent propositions to solve a problem
propositions.B: Large Language Models are used for other purposes than the suited one
propositions.C: The fact that deepfakes are weakening the relevance of AI itself
propositions.D: It cannot be as trustworthy as humans

questions.justification: The last paragraph states that true reasoning requires 'drawing inferences relevant to the solution of the particular problem. This is one of the hardest problems confronting AI' — exactly what A paraphrases.

questions.question: Studies found that "hallucinations" are mostly caused by:

propositions.A: A bad understanding of ChatGPT and the use of Large Language Models as generators
propositions.B: The fact that Large Language Models aren't used for their planned function

questions.justification: The text attributes hallucinations to 'using Large Language Models as search engines rather than in their intended role of text generation' — i.e. using them outside their planned function.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is the best definition of "hallucinations":

propositions.A: Hallucinations occur when Large Language Models generate erroneous or nonsensical responses
propositions.B: Hallucinations are the fact AI users are sometimes unable to understand outputs of their research
propositions.C: Hallucinations happen when Large Language Models understand perfectly the users' prompts
propositions.D: Hallucinations are mainly due to a bad internet connection

questions.justification: The text defines it as the model responding 'with probable but inaccurate text' instead of admitting it does not know — exactly what A describes as erroneous/nonsensical output.

questions.question: ChatGPT alarmed many intellectuals because:

propositions.C: Its answers to questions and statements are human-like

questions.justification: The text explains the concern was that 'it was impossible to distinguish human writing from ChatGPT-generated writing' — the human-like quality of its output, matching C.

questions.question: Which statement about deepfakes is INCORRECT:

propositions.A: It is generally an altered video, image or audio
propositions.C: It's impossible to refine fake content
propositions.D: Deepfakes are mainly created for blackmail and reputation damage

questions.justification: The text describes deepfake algorithms as an iterative process of creating and refining the 'best possible replica' — so refining fake content is exactly what does happen, contradicting C.

questions.question: These videos are used to extort a victim means:

propositions.A: They are used to threaten victims and force them to give money
propositions.B: They are used to tarnish a victim's reputation

questions.justification: The text lists 'extort a victim, ruin a person's reputation, get revenge or simply cyberbullying' as separate, distinct purposes — 'extort' specifically corresponds to the classic sense of threatening someone to obtain money (A), while 'ruin a person's reputation' (B) is a different item in that same list.

questions.question: In the text, what is the answer to the question "What, then, is involved in genuine understanding"?

propositions.A: There are answers but they are not relevant
propositions.B: There are as many answers as respondents
propositions.C: There are answers but not a consent

questions.justification: The text answers with: 'There is no universally agreed answer to this difficult question' — i.e. answers exist but no consensus, matching C.

questions.question: …………… is any media that has been manipulated mostly maliciously in order to replicate people.
questions.justification: This is precisely the definition of a deepfake given in the text: AI-generated media that swaps or replicates a person.

questions.question: …………… is an AI tool designed for communication. It can elaborate written and oral interactions or conversations.
questions.justification: A chatbot is, by definition, an AI application designed to hold written or spoken conversations, cited in the text as one of AI's applications.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines AI:
questions.justification: The text's opening definition covers reasoning, problem-solving, learning and data-driven applications — AI is described broadly enough to encompass all three partial definitions.

questions.question: The "Father of AI" is:
questions.justification: John McCarthy coined the term 'artificial intelligence' in 1956 and is widely credited as the father of the field — general knowledge, not stated in the text itself.

questions.question: When was ChatGPT officially released?
questions.justification: ChatGPT was publicly released by OpenAI in November 2022 — general knowledge.

questions.question: Which country created ChatGPT?
questions.justification: ChatGPT was created by OpenAI, a company based in the United States — general knowledge.

questions.question: When was the term Artificial Intelligence coined?
questions.justification: The term was coined in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference — general knowledge.

questions.question: Which of these applications function with AI?
questions.justification: Google Maps (route prediction), Siri and Bixby (voice assistants) all rely on AI technologies — general knowledge.
